name: cryptofeed
description: Cryptofeed - Real-time cryptocurrency market data feeds from 40+ exchanges. WebSocket streaming, normalized data, order books, trades, tickers. Python library for algorithmic trading and market data analysis.
capabilities: Connect to 40+ cryptocurrency exchanges via WebSocket
Process real-time trades, tickers, and order book updates
Normalize data across exchanges for consistent handling
Aggregate NBBO feeds for cross-exchange arbitrage
Write data directly to backends like Redis, Kafka, MongoDB
Handle authenticated channels for private account data
author: 2025Emma
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with guidance for using the Cryptofeed Python library. Static findings are false positives: markdown backticks flagged as shell execution, documentation URLs as network access, and crypto terminology as weak algorithms. No executable code, scripts, or actual security risks present.
valueStatement: Developers need a reliable way to stream real-time cryptocurrency data from multiple exchanges. Cryptofeed provides normalized WebSocket feeds from 40+ exchanges, simplifying algorithmic trading and market data analysis.
userTitle: Build crypto trading systems with Cryptofeed
limitations: Only provides data feeds, does not execute trades

useCases.title: Multi-exchange price monitoring
useCases.description: Aggregate real-time prices from Binance, Coinbase, and Kraken to identify arbitrage opportunities.

useCases.title: Market data pipelines
useCases.description: Stream exchange data directly to Redis, Kafka, or MongoDB for downstream analytics.

useCases.title: Order book analysis
useCases.description: Capture and analyze L2/L3 order book depth and trade flows for strategy research.

promptTemplates.title: Basic connection
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to set up a FeedHandler to stream ticker data from Coinbase for BTC-USD using Cryptofeed.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-feed setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I subscribe to trade data from both Binance and Bitfinex simultaneously using Cryptofeed?

promptTemplates.title: NBBO aggregation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an NBBO feed that aggregates the best bid and ask from Coinbase, Kraken, and Gemini for BTC-USD.

promptTemplates.title: Backend integration
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I configure Cryptofeed to write incoming ticker data directly to Redis or Kafka?

bestPractices: Use virtual environments to isolate Cryptofeed and its dependencies
Implement reconnection logic to handle exchange disconnections gracefully
Subscribe only to necessary channels to minimize bandwidth and rate limit usage
antiPatterns: Running multiple FeedHandlers for the same exchange simultaneously
Ignoring error callbacks and connection failure notifications
Hardcoding API keys directly in callback functions or scripts

faq.question: Which exchanges does Cryptofeed support?
faq.answer: Cryptofeed supports 40+ exchanges including Binance, Coinbase, Kraken, Bitfinex, Gemini, OKX, Bybit, and many others.

faq.question: What data channels are available?
faq.answer: Public channels include TRADES, TICKER, L1/L2/L3_BOOK, FUNDING, OPEN_INTEREST, LIQUIDATIONS, INDEX, and CANDLES.

faq.question: Can I use authenticated endpoints?
faq.answer: Yes, authenticated channels support ORDER_INFO, TRANSACTIONS, BALANCES, and FILLS for private account data.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this library?
faq.answer: Cryptofeed only reads public data or your authenticated credentials locally. No data is transmitted to third parties.

faq.question: What backends can I write data to?
faq.answer: Supported backends include Redis, MongoDB, Kafka, InfluxDB, PostgreSQL, Arctic, ZeroMQ, and GCP Pub/Sub.

faq.question: How does Cryptofeed compare to CCXT?
faq.answer: CCXT focuses on REST API trading. Cryptofeed specializes in WebSocket streaming with normalized data across exchanges.
